name: Marshmallow Easter Eggs2
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the gelatin in the top of a double boiler and add cold water. When the gelatin has softened, add boiling water and stir well. Add sugar and salt.
2. Heat over boiling water until the sugar dissolves completely. Remove from heat.
3. Pour into a large bowl fitted with an electric mixer and beat at high speed until thick but not stiff like beaten egg whites. Add vanilla.
4. Meanwhile, spread 2 inches of flour in a large pan. Push an egg (in shell) into the flour at intervals to create hollow spaces for molding marshmallow mixture.
5. Drop marshmallow mixture into the flour molds. Sprinkle flour lightly over top and refrigerate until set.
6. Remove marshmallow from one mold to form a half-egg. Trim flat side of marshmallow to make it even.
7. Dip the rounded part of the half-egg in melted chocolate, allowing it to cool on the flat (uncoated) side down.
8. Trim the flat side of the second half-egg, lift from the mold, and coat completely with chocolate.
9. Press the flat side of the coated second half against the cooled half-egg's flat side. The chocolate will hold them together.
10. When the chocolate-coated eggs are cool, use Regal Icing to decorate by making ruffles around them to cover the seam where halves join and add decoration. Write names or press designs with fine tips of a cake decorator tube.
11. Frosting may be left white or tinted in pastel colors. This recipe makes 13 whole eggs (or 26 half-eggs).
